seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Gloucester Tops and Goat Island. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Gloucester Tops vs Goat Island: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Gloucester Tops and Goat Island.
heading: Gloucester Tops vs Goat Island — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Gloucester Tops, New South Wales and Goat Island, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Goat Island has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Gloucester Tops (42/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Gloucester Tops or Goat Island safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Gloucester Tops has a flood risk score of 40/100, while Goat Island scores 27/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Gloucester Tops scores 55/100 for bushfire risk. Goat Island scores 46/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Gloucester Tops or Goat Island?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Goat Island has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.73% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
